preamble: The development of a voiced dental stop into a voiced postalveolar affricate, often via yod-coalescence (d + j > dʒ).

examples.note: Allophonic palatalization where /d/ is realized as an affricate [dʒ] before /i/ or /j/ (e.g. 'ji' [dʒi]).
Yod-coalescence where /d/ and /j/ merge into the affricate [dʒ].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
